qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in include/render tags allows arbitrary file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the exact vulnerable code/root cause described in advisory GHSA-wmfp-5q7x-987x: LiquidJS allows require.resolve() fallback without limiting directories (src/fs/fs.ts:12-13 and src/fs/loader.ts:32 where contains() defaults to true), enabling path traversal in include/render/layout tags to read arbitrary files. The finding documents the same vulnerable components (include.ts, render.ts, fs.ts, loader.ts), same CWE-22 path traversal class, and demonstrates the exact impact (arbitrary file read) described in the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Path traversal in include/render tags allows arbitrary file read

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: LiquidJS's contains() function in src/fs/fs.ts:12-13 and src/fs/loader.ts:32 defaults to returning true, bypassing path containment validation and allowing arbitrary file read through include/render/layout tags. The PoC successfully executed and demonstrated exploit success: reading /etc/passwd content via {% include "../../../../../../../../../../../../../etc/passwd" %}, confirming the advisory's claimed impact of arbitrary file access. The runtime artifacts satisfy the exploit success predicate with proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESSFUL and actual passwd file content in the output, establishing Tier1 end-to-end exploit success.
evidence: Finding vulnerability report documents src/fs/fs.ts:12-13 where contains() 'always return true by default' with explicit warning about path traversal exposure
Finding vulnerability report documents src/fs/loader.ts:32 setting this.contains = this.options.fs.contains || (() => true), confirming default insecure behavior
PoC executed successfully with exit code 0, demonstrating path traversal via LiquidJS include tag
Run log shows actual /etc/passwd content including 'nobody:*:-2:-2:Unpri', confirming arbitrary file read
Proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESSFUL emitted, matching expected exploit success signal
Finding CVE references (CVE-2026-30952) aligns with advisory aliases
Finding CWE-22 (path traversal) matches advisory database_specific cwe_ids

poc_executions.observed_effect: The PoC successfully exploited the path traversal vulnerability in LiquidJS 10.24.0. Using {% include "../../../../../../../../../../../../../etc/passwd" %}, the template engine read /etc/passwd content and displayed it in the output. The run log shows the actual passwd file content including 'nobody:*:-2:-2:Unpri' and other entries, confirming arbitrary file read. The proof string EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESSFUL was emitted, matching the expected exploit success signal. This demonstrates that attacker-controlled input in include/render tags can traverse outside the template root directory and read arbitrary files on the filesystem, which is the exact vulnerability described in the advisory.
